server.name: microsoft/markitdown
server.description: Convert various file formats (PDF, Word, Excel, images, audio) to Markdown.

server.description: MCP server for Chrome DevTools

server.name: microsoft/playwright-mcp
server.description: Automate web browsers using accessibility trees for testing and data extraction.

server.name: io.github.github/github-mcp-server

runtime_arguments.name: -e

headers.description: Authorization header with authentication token (PAT or App token)

server.name: oraios/serena
server.description: Semantic code retrieval & editing tools for coding agents.

server.name: coplaydev/unity-mcp
server.description: Control the Unity Editor from MCP clients via a Unity bridge + local Python server.

unity_mcp_server_src.description: Absolute path to the Unity MCP Server 'src' folder installed by the Unity package (e.g., macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/UnityMCP/UnityMcpServer/src; Windows: %USERPROFILE%/AppData/Local/UnityMCP/UnityMcpServer/src; Linux: ~/.local/share/UnityMCP/UnityMcpServer/src).

server.name: firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server
server.description: Extract web data with Firecrawl

server.name: io.github.wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander
server.description: MCP server for terminal commands, file operations, and process management

server.name: makenotion/notion-mcp-server
server.description: Official MCP server for Notion API

server.name: com.microsoft/azure
server.description: All Azure MCP tools to create a seamless connection between AI agents and Azure services.

server.name: com.microsoft/microsoft-fabric
server.description: MCP tools for interacting with Microsoft Fabric

server.name: io.github.bytebase/dbhub
server.description: Minimal, token-efficient Database MCP Server for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, SQLite, MariaDB

environment_variables.description: Database connection string (e.g., postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db)
environment_variables.name: DSN

environment_variables.description: Database type: postgres, mysql, mariadb, sqlserver, sqlite
environment_variables.name: DB_TYPE

environment_variables.description: Database hostname
environment_variables.name: DB_HOST

environment_variables.description: Database port
environment_variables.name: DB_PORT

environment_variables.description: Database username
environment_variables.name: DB_USER

environment_variables.description: Database password
environment_variables.name: DB_PASSWORD

environment_variables.description: Database name or SQLite file path
environment_variables.name: DB_NAME

environment_variables.description: Transport mode: stdio or http
environment_variables.name: TRANSPORT

environment_variables.description: HTTP server port (default: 8080)
environment_variables.name: PORT

environment_variables.description: Instance identifier for tool names
environment_variables.name: ID

environment_variables.description: SSH server hostname
environment_variables.name: SSH_HOST

environment_variables.description: SSH server port (default: 22)
environment_variables.name: SSH_PORT

environment_variables.description: SSH username
environment_variables.name: SSH_USER

environment_variables.description: SSH password
environment_variables.name: SSH_PASSWORD

environment_variables.description: Path to SSH private key
environment_variables.name: SSH_KEY

environment_variables.description: SSH key passphrase
environment_variables.name: SSH_PASSPHRASE

server.name: com.supabase/mcp
server.description: MCP server for interacting with the Supabase platform

environment_variables.description: Personal access token for Supabase API
environment_variables.name: SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN

runtime_arguments.description: Comma-separated list of features to enable
runtime_arguments.name: --features

server.name: io.github.brightdata/brightdata-mcp
server.description: Bright Data's Web MCP server enabling AI agents to search, extract & navigate the web

environment_variables.description: Your API key for Bright Data
environment_variables.name: API_TOKEN

environment_variables.description: Your unlocker zone name
environment_variables.name: WEB_UNLOCKER_ZONE

environment_variables.description: Your browser zone name
environment_variables.name: BROWSER_ZONE

environment_variables.description: To enable PRO_MODE - set to true
environment_variables.name: PRO_MODE

environment_variables.description: Comma-separated tool group IDs (e.g., 'ecommerce,browser')
environment_variables.name: GROUPS

environment_variables.description: Comma-separated tool names to enable
environment_variables.name: TOOLS

environment_variables.description: Polling timeout in seconds for web_data_* tools (default: 600)
environment_variables.name: POLLING_TIMEOUT

environment_variables.description: Request timeout in seconds for base tools (search & scrape). No limit if unset
environment_variables.name: BASE_TIMEOUT

environment_variables.description: Max retries for base tools on transient errors (0-3, default: 0)
environment_variables.name: BASE_MAX_RETRIES

server.name: io.github.tavily-ai/tavily-mcp
server.description: MCP server for advanced web search using Tavily

environment_variables.description: Your Tavily API key from tavily.com
environment_variables.name: TAVILY_API_KEY

environment_variables.description: JSON string with default search parameters (e.g., {"include_images": true, "max_results": 15})
environment_variables.name: DEFAULT_PARAMETERS

server.name: microsoft/azure-devops-mcp
server.description: Interact with Azure DevOps services like repositories, work items, builds, releases, test plans, and code search.

ado_domain.description: Repeat to enable specific domains: core, work, work-items, search, test-plans, repositories, wiki, pipelines, advanced-security.

server.name: microsoftdocs/mcp
server.description: Enables clients like GitHub Copilot and other AI agents to bring trusted and up-to-date information directly from Microsoft's official documentation.

server.name: com.stripe/mcp
server.description: MCP server integrating with Stripe - tools for customers, products, payments, and more.

server.name: com.figma.mcp/mcp
server.description: The Figma MCP server brings Figma design context directly into your AI workflow.

server.name: com.microsoft/nuget
server.description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for NuGet.

runtime_arguments.name: -e

runtime_arguments.description: HCP Terraform or Terraform Enterprise API token used to authenticate requests.
runtime_arguments.name: -e

runtime_arguments.description: Set to true to enable tools that execute Terraform operations requiring explicit approval.
runtime_arguments.name: -e

server.name: com.apify/apify-mcp-server
server.description: Extract data from any website with thousands of scrapers, crawlers, and automations on Apify Store ⚡

headers.description: Apify API token for authentication with Apify platform services. For example 'Bearer <apify-api-token>'

server.name: io.github.mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server
server.description: MongoDB Model Context Protocol Server

environment_variables.description: When set to true, allows configuration values to be overridden via request headers and query parameters.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_ALLOW_REQUEST_OVERRIDES

environment_variables.description: Atlas API client ID for authentication. Required for running Atlas tools.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_API_CLIENT_ID

environment_variables.description: Atlas API client secret for authentication. Required for running Atlas tools.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_API_CLIENT_SECRET

environment_variables.description: Base URL for the MongoDB Assistant API.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_ASSISTANT_BASE_URL

environment_variables.description: Time in milliseconds that temporary database users created when connecting to MongoDB Atlas clusters will remain active before being automatically deleted.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_ATLAS_TEMPORARY_DATABASE_USER_LIFETIME_MS

environment_variables.description: Comma separated values of tool names that require user confirmation before execution. Requires the client to support elicitation.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED_TOOLS

environment_variables.description: MongoDB connection string for direct database connections. Optional, if not set, you'll need to call the connect tool before interacting with MongoDB data.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_CONNECTION_STRING

environment_variables.description: Comma separated values of tool names, operation types, and/or categories of tools that will be disabled.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS

environment_variables.description: When true, runs the server in dry mode: dumps configuration and enabled tools, then exits without starting the server.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_DRY_RUN

environment_variables.description: Time in milliseconds between export cleanup cycles that remove expired export files.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_EXPORT_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS

environment_variables.description: Time in milliseconds after which an export is considered expired and eligible for cleanup.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_EXPORT_TIMEOUT_MS

environment_variables.description: Folder to store exported data files.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_EXPORTS_PATH

environment_variables.description: When true, the HTTP transport allows requests with a session ID supplied externally through the 'mcp-session-id' header. When an external ID is supplied, the initialization request is optional.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_EXTERNALLY_MANAGED_SESSIONS

environment_variables.description: Deprecated. Use `monitoringServerHost` instead. Host address to bind the healthCheck HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `healthCheckPort` must also be set.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HEALTH_CHECK_HOST

environment_variables.description: Deprecated. Use `monitoringServerPort` instead. Port number for the healthCheck HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `healthCheckHost` must also be set.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HEALTH_CHECK_PORT

environment_variables.description: Maximum size of the HTTP request body in bytes (only used when transport is 'http'). This value is passed as the optional limit parameter to the Express.js json() middleware.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HTTP_BODY_LIMIT

environment_variables.description: Header that the HTTP server will validate when making requests (only used when transport is 'http').
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HTTP_HEADERS

environment_variables.description: Host address to bind the HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http').
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HTTP_HOST

environment_variables.description: Port number for the HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). Use 0 for a random port.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HTTP_PORT

environment_variables.description: The HTTP response type for tool responses: 'sse' for Server-Sent Events, 'json' for standard JSON responses.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_HTTP_RESPONSE_TYPE

environment_variables.description: Idle timeout for a client to disconnect (only applies to http transport).
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS

environment_variables.description: When set to true, enforces that query operations must use an index, rejecting queries that perform a collection scan.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_INDEX_CHECK

environment_variables.description: Folder to store logs.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_LOG_PATH

environment_variables.description: Comma separated values of logger types.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_LOGGERS

environment_variables.description: The maximum size in bytes for results from a find or aggregate tool call. This serves as an upper bound for the responseBytesLimit parameter in those tools.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MAX_BYTES_PER_QUERY

environment_variables.description: The maximum number of documents that can be returned by a find or aggregate tool call. For the find tool, the effective limit will be the smaller of this value and the tool's limit parameter.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MAX_DOCUMENTS_PER_QUERY

environment_variables.description: The maximum time in milliseconds that operations are allowed to run on the MongoDB server. When set, this value is passed as the maxTimeMS option to read operations such as find, aggregate, and count.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MAX_TIME_M_S

environment_variables.description: Minimum severity level for log messages forwarded to the MCP client.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MCP_CLIENT_LOG_LEVEL

environment_variables.description: Features to expose on the monitoring server (only used when transport is 'http' and monitoringServerHost/monitoringServerPort are set).
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MONITORING_SERVER_FEATURES

environment_variables.description: Host address to bind the monitoring HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerPort` must also be set.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MONITORING_SERVER_HOST

environment_variables.description: Port number for the monitoring HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerHost` must also be set.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_MONITORING_SERVER_PORT

environment_variables.description: Notification timeout for a client to be aware of disconnect (only applies to http transport).
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_NOTIFICATION_TIMEOUT_MS

environment_variables.description: Comma separated values of preview features that are enabled.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_PREVIEW_FEATURES

environment_variables.description: When set to true, only allows read, connect, and metadata operation types, disabling create/update/delete operations.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_READ_ONLY

environment_variables.description: When set to disabled, disables telemetry collection.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_TELEMETRY

environment_variables.description: Either 'stdio' or 'http'.
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_TRANSPORT

environment_variables.description: API key for Voyage AI embeddings service (required for creating Atlas Local deployments with auto-embed vector search capabilities).
environment_variables.name: MDB_MCP_VOYAGE_API_KEY

package_arguments.description: When set to true, allows configuration values to be overridden via request headers and query parameters.
package_arguments.name: --allowRequestOverrides

package_arguments.description: Atlas API client ID for authentication. Required for running Atlas tools.
package_arguments.name: --apiClientId

package_arguments.description: Atlas API client secret for authentication. Required for running Atlas tools.
package_arguments.name: --apiClientSecret

package_arguments.description: Base URL for the MongoDB Assistant API.
package_arguments.name: --assistantBaseUrl

package_arguments.description: Time in milliseconds that temporary database users created when connecting to MongoDB Atlas clusters will remain active before being automatically deleted.
package_arguments.name: --atlasTemporaryDatabaseUserLifetimeMs

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of tool names that require user confirmation before execution. Requires the client to support elicitation.
package_arguments.name: --confirmationRequiredTools

package_arguments.description: MongoDB connection string for direct database connections. Optional, if not set, you'll need to call the connect tool before interacting with MongoDB data.
package_arguments.name: --connectionString

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of tool names, operation types, and/or categories of tools that will be disabled.
package_arguments.name: --disabledTools

package_arguments.description: When true, runs the server in dry mode: dumps configuration and enabled tools, then exits without starting the server.
package_arguments.name: --dryRun

package_arguments.description: Time in milliseconds between export cleanup cycles that remove expired export files.
package_arguments.name: --exportCleanupIntervalMs

package_arguments.description: Time in milliseconds after which an export is considered expired and eligible for cleanup.
package_arguments.name: --exportTimeoutMs

package_arguments.description: Folder to store exported data files.
package_arguments.name: --exportsPath

package_arguments.description: When true, the HTTP transport allows requests with a session ID supplied externally through the 'mcp-session-id' header. When an external ID is supplied, the initialization request is optional.
package_arguments.name: --externallyManagedSessions

package_arguments.description: Deprecated. Use `monitoringServerHost` instead. Host address to bind the healthCheck HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `healthCheckPort` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --healthCheckHost

package_arguments.description: Deprecated. Use `monitoringServerPort` instead. Port number for the healthCheck HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `healthCheckHost` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --healthCheckPort

package_arguments.description: Maximum size of the HTTP request body in bytes (only used when transport is 'http'). This value is passed as the optional limit parameter to the Express.js json() middleware.
package_arguments.name: --httpBodyLimit

package_arguments.description: Header that the HTTP server will validate when making requests (only used when transport is 'http').
package_arguments.name: --httpHeaders

package_arguments.description: Host address to bind the HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http').
package_arguments.name: --httpHost

package_arguments.description: Port number for the HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). Use 0 for a random port.
package_arguments.name: --httpPort

package_arguments.description: The HTTP response type for tool responses: 'sse' for Server-Sent Events, 'json' for standard JSON responses.
package_arguments.name: --httpResponseType

package_arguments.description: Idle timeout for a client to disconnect (only applies to http transport).
package_arguments.name: --idleTimeoutMs

package_arguments.description: When set to true, enforces that query operations must use an index, rejecting queries that perform a collection scan.
package_arguments.name: --indexCheck

package_arguments.description: Folder to store logs.
package_arguments.name: --logPath

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of logger types.
package_arguments.name: --loggers

package_arguments.description: The maximum size in bytes for results from a find or aggregate tool call. This serves as an upper bound for the responseBytesLimit parameter in those tools.
package_arguments.name: --maxBytesPerQuery

package_arguments.description: The maximum number of documents that can be returned by a find or aggregate tool call. For the find tool, the effective limit will be the smaller of this value and the tool's limit parameter.
package_arguments.name: --maxDocumentsPerQuery

package_arguments.description: The maximum time in milliseconds that operations are allowed to run on the MongoDB server. When set, this value is passed as the maxTimeMS option to read operations such as find, aggregate, and count.
package_arguments.name: --maxTimeMS

package_arguments.description: Minimum severity level for log messages forwarded to the MCP client.
package_arguments.name: --mcpClientLogLevel

package_arguments.description: Features to expose on the monitoring server (only used when transport is 'http' and monitoringServerHost/monitoringServerPort are set).
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerFeatures

package_arguments.description: Host address to bind the monitoring HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerPort` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerHost

package_arguments.description: Port number for the monitoring HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerHost` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerPort

package_arguments.description: Notification timeout for a client to be aware of disconnect (only applies to http transport).
package_arguments.name: --notificationTimeoutMs

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of preview features that are enabled.
package_arguments.name: --previewFeatures

package_arguments.description: When set to true, only allows read, connect, and metadata operation types, disabling create/update/delete operations.
package_arguments.name: --readOnly

package_arguments.description: When set to disabled, disables telemetry collection.
package_arguments.name: --telemetry

package_arguments.description: Either 'stdio' or 'http'.
package_arguments.name: --transport

package_arguments.description: API key for Voyage AI embeddings service (required for creating Atlas Local deployments with auto-embed vector search capabilities).
package_arguments.name: --voyageApiKey

package_arguments.description: Maximum size of the HTTP request body in bytes (only used when transport is 'http'). This value is passed as the optional limit parameter to the Express.js json() middleware.
package_arguments.name: --httpBodyLimit

package_arguments.description: Header that the HTTP server will validate when making requests (only used when transport is 'http').
package_arguments.name: --httpHeaders

package_arguments.description: Host address to bind the HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http').
package_arguments.name: --httpHost

package_arguments.description: Port number for the HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). Use 0 for a random port.
package_arguments.name: --httpPort

package_arguments.description: The HTTP response type for tool responses: 'sse' for Server-Sent Events, 'json' for standard JSON responses.
package_arguments.name: --httpResponseType

package_arguments.description: Idle timeout for a client to disconnect (only applies to http transport).
package_arguments.name: --idleTimeoutMs

package_arguments.description: When set to true, enforces that query operations must use an index, rejecting queries that perform a collection scan.
package_arguments.name: --indexCheck

package_arguments.description: Folder to store logs.
package_arguments.name: --logPath

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of logger types.
package_arguments.name: --loggers

package_arguments.description: The maximum size in bytes for results from a find or aggregate tool call. This serves as an upper bound for the responseBytesLimit parameter in those tools.
package_arguments.name: --maxBytesPerQuery

package_arguments.description: The maximum number of documents that can be returned by a find or aggregate tool call. For the find tool, the effective limit will be the smaller of this value and the tool's limit parameter.
package_arguments.name: --maxDocumentsPerQuery

package_arguments.description: The maximum time in milliseconds that operations are allowed to run on the MongoDB server. When set, this value is passed as the maxTimeMS option to read operations such as find, aggregate, and count.
package_arguments.name: --maxTimeMS

package_arguments.description: Minimum severity level for log messages forwarded to the MCP client.
package_arguments.name: --mcpClientLogLevel

package_arguments.description: Features to expose on the monitoring server (only used when transport is 'http' and monitoringServerHost/monitoringServerPort are set).
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerFeatures

package_arguments.description: Host address to bind the monitoring HTTP server to (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerPort` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerHost

package_arguments.description: Port number for the monitoring HTTP server (only used when transport is 'http'). If provided, `monitoringServerHost` must also be set.
package_arguments.name: --monitoringServerPort

package_arguments.description: Notification timeout for a client to be aware of disconnect (only applies to http transport).
package_arguments.name: --notificationTimeoutMs

package_arguments.description: Comma separated values of preview features that are enabled.
package_arguments.name: --previewFeatures

package_arguments.description: When set to true, only allows read, connect, and metadata operation types, disabling create/update/delete operations.
package_arguments.name: --readOnly

package_arguments.description: When set to disabled, disables telemetry collection.
package_arguments.name: --telemetry

package_arguments.description: Either 'stdio' or 'http'.
package_arguments.name: --transport

package_arguments.description: API key for Voyage AI embeddings service (required for creating Atlas Local deployments with auto-embed vector search capabilities).
package_arguments.name: --voyageApiKey
